projectDescription: A deep learning binary classification project that predicts heart disease presence using the UCI Heart Disease dataset, exploring neural network architectures, activation functions, optimizers, and hyperparameters.
keyFeatures: Binary classification neural network for heart disease prediction
Systematic comparison of activation functions (ReLU, Tanh, Sigmoid, Leaky ReLU)
Batch size impact study on generalization
Dropout regularization to prevent overfitting on small dataset
~97-99% test accuracy using optimal ReLU + Adam + lr=0.001 configuration
Uses UCI Heart Disease dataset with 1,025 patient records and 13 clinical features
publicApiSurface: build_model(activation, optimizer, dropout_rate) — constructs and compiles the Keras Sequential model
build_deep_model() — 5 hidden layer deep architecture for comparison
plot_history(history, title) — plots training/validation loss and accuracy curves
heart.csv — UCI Heart Disease dataset with 1,025 samples and 13 clinical features
StandardScaler — feature normalization applied before training
